content: Anxiety started when I was invited to that dinner, therapist. I felt like everyone was already judging me, like they were already thinking I’m dumb or not good enough for this job. I kept smiling and agreeing with what others said even when I didn’t feel it myself, just to seem friendly or interesting. But then, I laughed at some jokes that weren’t funny, and I agreed to things I didn’t think would work, just to fit in. It’s exhausting because I want to show I’m interested, but I’m scared someone might think I’m shallow or not true to my place. I guess I just don’t trust myself to speak up or make a difference. It’s like no matter what I do, it feels inevitable that I’ll fall behind or be seen as strange or incompetent. I’d love for someone to recognize that I’m struggling with these feelings, but I’m not sure how to say it. It’s hard to admit that I need help or support when I’m so convinced I’m stuck in this loop. So, I keep pretending everything goes according to plan, but deep down, I’m still feeling like maybe I just don’t belong here. Just knowing there’s something wrong doesn’t give me the courage to change my mind or face the reality of being stuck like this. It’s frustrating, but I’m afraid to face the truth. What I’m hoping to hear is that talking about these doubts isn’t going to solve anything, but I can’t resist the urge to tell someone how I feel. It’s like being honest with myself feels too painful, even though honesty is the least I could expect from anyone else. So, I end up just saying “I’m sorry,” which feels polite but also untrue. I prefer to pretend than actually experience the real thing, and I don’t think that’s really going to change anything. I hope we can talk about

Yeah, it’s just like that last week—no one seems to care about me anymore when I’m around. I keep thinking, “What’s going on? Why aren’t they liking me?” I just feel... invisible, like I’m just there, needed, but nothing. I try to explain everything away, but then they just start talking too much, and I end up feeling even more lost and alone. It’s hard to say what I’m doing wrong when I know I’m definitely messing things up. I guess I just don’t trust myself to stay connected because I’m afraid to ask for help or show I need anything. It’s like no matter how many times I try, it never works out the way I hoped. So, I just choose to pull back and let go. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to stop feeling so stuck. I’d appreciate it if you could see through me and make me realize I’m not just making a mistake. Thanks for giving me a chance to describe this to you. It’s really tough to imagine how I’ll fix this without actually facing it.

Yeah, the new project... it just felt like everything was falling apart right away. I kept thinking, “What’s going to happen if I don’t get this perfect?” I’ve been staying late reviewing my work too much, but honestly, it just makes me feel worse instead of helping. I keep putting things off because I’m scared of embarrassing myself or proving I’m not cut out for this job. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I try harder, but it only makes things worse. I guess part of me knows that maybe I’m not really as capable after all, but I’m scared to say anything. It’s like I’m afraid to face the possibility of failing unless I’m already prepared to suffer the consequences. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out how to start this without telling me first—I just need some guidance. I don’t know where to begin. Please? Thanks for asking. I’ll try to be less comfortable trying. Help would really make a difference to me. Thank you so much for inviting me on this therapy session. It’s been great having you here. I look forward to our discussion. 😄 I love being weird and stubborn enough to think deeply about my feelings before coming to a decision. But honestly, sometimes I just freeze up. It’s frustrating because I want to express my true thoughts and emotions, but I’m usually scared to risk showing them. I hope we can find a way to let go of those lingering doubts. Thank you again for inviting me to join this important conversation. I appreciate you laughing with me. It just makes me feel so special. 💬 Let me know if there’s anything else I can tell you. 👤

Yeah, I guess I was really looking forward to this meeting, but then when I got there, I just felt like everything was already set up, you know? I kept thinking maybe they were all just quieting down because I was being too talkative. I didn’t want to bother anyone or seem nervous, so I mostly stayed on the periphery of the group. It’s like I’m scared to show my feelings or admit I need help unless it’s absolutely necessary. I’ll probably be late if I don’t get away earlier. I feel pretty out of place most of the time, which makes me even more anxious at these kinds of events. It’s frustrating because I want to join in, but I just freeze up. Just being there makes me feel like I’m invisible or unworthy. I’m sorry I wasn’t more comfortable. It’s hard to change things that feel stuck and predictable. Can you imagine what it would be like to have a more relaxed approach to those kind of situations? I’d really appreciate it if you could see me that way. Well, this situation just hit me pretty hard. I remember back when we talked about it, I tried to say something about wanting more independence, but my partner just decided things on their own right away. It felt like, at first, I was like, “Why bother?” Then, I just shut down and agreed to whatever they said, even though I didn’t really want it. It’s like, no matter what I say, it’s never going to change how things go. I’m scared that if I try to push back, I’ll just make everything worse or get rejected again. It’s exhausting, knowing that I’m just stuck in this loop. I hope someone can see, but I don’t know how to start the conversation. Do you have any ideas? Or is this just going to stick around? Just saying no or telling me to stop trying won’t help much, I guess. So, I’m just going to pretend nothing happened and move on. It’s frustrating, because honestly, I’ve been thinking a lot about what I want for a while. But I’m not sure how to make it count. I’d love to fix this, but I’m not sure I’m capable of it. It’s like I’m stuck in this eternal struggle. I appreciate you playing leader here, but I’m tired of this. I need a new game plan soon. Please, let’s get talking and move beyond this. Thanks for laughing with me. I appreciate you noticing. Take care!

Yeah, it’s just like that at that family meeting last week. When they started talking about career and lifestyle plans, I felt this same sinking sensation, like I wasn’t going anywhere even though I’m already stuck. I didn’t really say much because I just wanted to get away from all of them right then. It’s like they have a plan that I can’t even hope to change, and I end up shutting down or becoming less involved. Honestly, it makes me feel very alone and useless, and I’ve been pretending not to feel these ways for a while. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I’m not being heard and then I just fall apart. I guess part of me feels resigned, kind of like I’m accepting something that won’t change, even if it means I’m not quite who I want to be. It’s exhausting, really. I just want someone to understand why I’m here, but I’m not sure how to bring it up. So, it feels safer to just stay quiet and leave. Can you imagine what those feelings might be like for me? I’m sorry I don’t have a better way to talk. It’s hard to keep the truth coming through when I’m just… not enough. Thank you so much for asking! I’ll try not to show how tough it must be for you. Yeah, the last dinner was like... another one of those pressure dinners. My dad was all set, talking about his job and plans for the future, and I’m here, just... holding back, okay? I felt like I wasn’t really part of whatever conversation was happening because I didn’t know what to say. Afterward, I’d just sit there thinking about it, feeling like maybe I’m being unfair or disappointing them somehow. It’s like no matter what I do, it always ends up wrong. I guess I just feel helpless and useless when things get this crazy. It’s hard to move on after having to go through with something I don’t want to do. I’m sorry I’ve done this before—I don’t really want to make it worse. Just knowing you’ll find out somewhere makes me even more unhappy. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want change, but it just feels impossible. I’m scared to ask for help because I’m afraid it’ll just prove how useless I am to my family. But if anyone else could handle this better, it’d be them. Thanks for giving me this chance to explain. I appreciate you for inviting me though. It’s important to me that I’m invited here, especially with people like you. Please let me know if there’s anything I can improve. Anxiety fills me up when our relationship gets tense. I feel like if I say something I don’t want to, it’ll just make everything worse or cause the other person to dislike me. So, I just stay quiet and keep agreeing with what my partner says because I don’t want to cause a fight. It’s like I’m afraid of being judged or being rejected, and I prefer that to facing those feelings directly. I guess I just know that keeping my own thoughts and feelings hidden makes me feel safer from a relationship perspective. It’s frustrating because I want to be strong and assertive, but I don’t know how to speak up when things get too rough. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out some way to stop letting me just adapt and fall further apart. Thanks for reminding me of you. It’s tough to connect with someone you don’t truly understand, but I need someone to believe in me enough to stick around. Please let me know if there’s anything I can do to change how this feels. Thank you for being here for me. I love you, and thank you for inviting me on this particular therapy session. I hope we can talk to each other better. Remember, it’s okay to ask for different things sometimes. I appreciate you being here, and I’ll be back next week. Love you, and sorry again. I’ve been to the family gathering twice lately, and every time I’m around people, I feel like I’m just invisible. It’s like no one really wants to join me—like they don’t even recognize me when I’m not there. I end up just sitting alone in another room, thinking maybe if I say something, they’ll start connecting with me somehow. It’s exhausting because I want to connect, but it always feels like I’m on the outside looking in. I keep reminding myself that I’m important, but how can I make things right again? It’s like I’m stuck in this never-ending cycle of feeling left out and rejected. I hope someone can help me figure out what I’m missing. It’s frustrating, and I just feel scared and worthless. Just knowing these feelings won’t go away, I’m afraid I’ll never be truly accepted anywhere.

Yeah, I looked at my budget last week, and honestly, it just didn’t feel right. I felt like I was falling down this financial ladder all over again, like I’m just not cut out for managing things properly. I kept thinking, “Maybe I’m just going to fail everywhere,” and then I’d spend hours worrying about whether I actually could afford something important instead of figuring out how to get better. It’s like when I look at the job posting for a loan and my brain just shuts down. I don’t want to face it anymore; it’s like I’m making a big deal out of it because it makes me feel worse. It’s scary to imagine if I didn’t cover my bills, what others would think about me. I guess I’m scared that if I start looking for jobs or trying different ways to handle money, I’ll just prove I’m incapable. It’s hard to say without feeling like I’m just not good enough for my own sake. I want to stop worrying and move forward, but I’m stuck where I am. What do you think?

Yeah, I guess I caught myself thinking that maybe I’m just not good enough when I’m away from home. I always try to hide what I’m going on, but then when they start complaining, I feel like maybe I’m causing trouble somehow. So, I tend to stay quiet or even say something silly that doesn’t really help. It’s like I’m there, but not really part of whatever we’re having. But then I just start working on making more time or trying to be nicer, and it only makes me feel worse. I’ve done that a few times, and honestly, it’s not helping at all. It’s like I’m there, but I’m invisible. It’s hard to connect with someone when I’m the one who thinks they don’t love me. I want to change things, but I’m stuck in this cycle where I just repeat what I already know. Can you ever see through that?

Well, since I got that offer to lead the new project, I’m really struggling to say yes. It feels like right now I’m already failing, and I don’t know what I’m capable of. I’ve been thinking a lot about putting together the presentation, and I’ve been working late just trying to make sure everything is perfect. But even then, I’m scared that people will notice my lack of experience and that I’m not cut out for this role. I feel like I’m always running the risk of making a mistake and getting noticed as badly. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want progress, but then I’m afraid of messing things up. I guess I just don’t trust myself to handle such big, important stuff yet. It’s exhausting playing games with myself like that. I’d much rather just be left behind—like most times in my life seem to end up doing. I’m sorry, I just can’t shake this feeling that I’m destined to fall apart. Just like sometimes people do in big jobs. It’s hard to stop thinking about how important this change is to them. I hope someone will see through me, though. I don’t want to lose this opportunity to be recognized or loved. It’s like I’m not ready for it yet. So, I guess I just need someone else to tell me what’s happening inside me. I’d love for there to be a breakthrough, but honestly, I’m just too scared to believe it. It’s like I’m never really ready for anything big to happen to me.

Yeah, that dinner was... kind of stressful for me. I knew I wanted to go to what felt favorite, but then when they suggested I chose the restaurant, I just froze. I kept thinking, “What if they think I’m too much?” So, I just stayed quiet and tried to hide what I really wanted. It’s like I don’t want them to feel uncomfortable or disappointed. I guess I just feel embarrassed all of a sudden when things don’t go well. Sometimes, playing nice helps, but honestly, it feels safer to stay on edge. I’d prefer not to be caught in these kinds of situations where I have to choose. It’s frustrating because I want to be liked or accepted. I didn’t want to say anything because it just made me seem weaker, and honestly, I’m ashamed that I’m stuck in this relationship where I don’t get to decide things. I left quickly after to avoid getting anymore stressed out. It’s like I’m failing at being an integral part of our family, so I end up feeling even more alone. I guess I just don’t know how to fix myself when I mess up in relationships. It’s like I’m always behind others’ expectations, and that makes me feel stuck.

Yeah, well, the conversation was really hard for me. I guess I was getting too attached to the idea of being alone because I didn’t want to lose them either. It’s like, if I keep pushing back, maybe they’ll figure out how much I actually care. Honestly, I’ve been feeling pretty stuck since then. It’s like someone’s just watching over me, preventing me from falling apart or losing them. I’m sorry I did this, but I feel like explaining things better than just pretending. What do you think?

I kept thinking maybe if I showed up again, people would actually recognize how useless I am. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not good enough.
